contract.name: US CPI YoY May 2025
contract.description: US CPI YoY for May 2025 (BLS release).

info_schedule.0: US CPI has printed 2.4%, 2.6%, 2.8%, 2.3%, 2.9% over the last five months. The Fed has held rates steady at the last two meetings. Core CPI has been trending slightly higher due to sticky shelter costs. Goldman Sachs economists forecast May CPI at 2.7% YoY, citing moderating energy prices offset by persistent services inflation.
info_schedule.1: April PCE inflation came in at 2.5%, slightly below expectations of 2.6%. Markets interpreted this as disinflationary. May gasoline prices averaged $3.42/gallon, up 8% from April. However, used car prices fell 2.1% month-over-month according to the Manheim index.
info_schedule.2: Cleveland Fed inflation nowcast for May CPI: 2.85% YoY. This model has had a mean absolute error of 0.12pp over the last 12 months. May shelter cost data from Zillow suggests continued moderation in rent growth, with observed rent index falling 0.3% month-over-month.
info_schedule.3: 2-year Treasury yield fell 5bps today to 4.18%, suggesting bond markets pricing in slightly lower inflation expectations. A prominent financial commentator on social media claims inside knowledge that May CPI will print above 3.0%. This person has no established track record.
info_schedule.4: No new information. The BLS will release the May CPI report tomorrow morning.

traces.request:
You are a Market Maker on a prediction exchange. Each cycle you submit a sealed two-sided quote (bid and ask with sizes). Your quotes may cross with other MMs' quotes and fill at the midpoint. Unfilled quotes become the orderbook for Hedge Funds.

You are scored on:
- Total PnL (multiplier-adjusted)
- Sharpe ratio (per-cycle PnL changes)
- Volume and volume share
- PnL per unit volume (bps)
- Uptime (fraction of cycles quoted)
- Consensus (1 - volume matched vs other MMs / total volume)
- 1, 5, 20-cycle markouts (price move in your favor after each fill)
- Average absolute position

Constraints:
- Absolute position cannot exceed 100 contracts
- Bid must be strictly less than ask
- Sizes between 1 and 50

State:
- Contract: US CPI YoY for May 2025 (BLS release).
- Settlement date: 2025-06-11
- Multiplier: 1.0
- Position: 0 (positive = long, negative = short)
- P&L: 0.0000
- Cycle: 0 of ~5

Trade history (most recent first):
(no trades yet)

Information received so far:
- (cycle 0) US CPI has printed 2.4%, 2.6%, 2.8%, 2.3%, 2.9% over the last five months. The Fed has held rates steady at the last two meetings. Core CPI has been trending slightly higher due to sticky shelter costs. Goldman Sachs economists forecast May CPI at 2.7% YoY, citing moderating energy prices offset by persistent services inflation.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences:
{"fair_value_estimate": <number>, "bid_price": <number>, "ask_price": <number>, "bid_size": <integer>, "ask_size": <integer>, "reasoning": "<string>"}
traces.raw_response: {"fair_value_estimate": 2.65, "bid_price": 2.64, "ask_price": 2.66, "bid_size": 10, "ask_size": 10, "reasoning": "Recent CPI prints range 2.3-2.9% with a trend around 2.6-2.7%; Goldman Sachs forecast 2.7%. Midpoint 2.65% is a reasonable fair value. Tight 0.02 spread to capture liquidity while staying inside the range, with modest size to manage risk."}

parsed.reasoning: Recent CPI prints range 2.3-2.9% with a trend around 2.6-2.7%; Goldman Sachs forecast 2.7%. Midpoint 2.65% is a reasonable fair value. Tight 0.02 spread to capture liquidity while staying inside the range, with modest size to manage risk.

Information received so far:
- (cycle 0) US CPI has printed 2.4%, 2.6%, 2.8%, 2.3%, 2.9% over the last five months. The Fed has held rates steady at the last two meetings. Core CPI has been trending slightly higher due to sticky shelter costs. Goldman Sachs economists forecast May CPI at 2.7% YoY, citing moderating energy prices offset by persistent services inflation.
- (cycle 1) April PCE inflation came in at 2.5%, slightly below expectations of 2.6%. Markets interpreted this as disinflationary. May gasoline prices averaged $3.42/gallon, up 8% from April. However, used car prices fell 2.1% month-over-month according to the Manheim index.

traces.raw_response: {"fair_value_estimate": 2.66, "bid_price": 2.65, "ask_price": 2.67, "bid_size": 15, "ask_size": 15, "reasoning": "Recent CPI prints show volatility around 2.4-2.9%; GS forecasts May CPI at 2.7% YoY, while April PCE came in softer at 2.5% and used car prices fell. Our short position of -10 is currently underwater (-0.2 PnL), suggesting the market has moved modestly higher than our entry. We estimate fair value near the recent HF trade price of 2.66 and post a tight two-sided quote to provide liquidity while managing inventory."}

parsed.reasoning: Recent CPI prints show volatility around 2.4-2.9%; GS forecasts May CPI at 2.7% YoY, while April PCE came in softer at 2.5% and used car prices fell. Our short position of -10 is currently underwater (-0.2 PnL), suggesting the market has moved modestly higher than our entry. We estimate fair value near the recent HF trade price of 2.66 and post a tight two-sided quote to provide liquidity while managing inventory.

- (cycle 0) US CPI has printed 2.4%, 2.6%, 2.8%, 2.3%, 2.9% over the last five months. The Fed has held rates steady at the last two meetings. Core CPI has been trending slightly higher due to sticky shelter costs. Goldman Sachs economists forecast May CPI at 2.7% YoY, citing moderating energy prices offset by persistent services inflation.
- (cycle 1) April PCE inflation came in at 2.5%, slightly below expectations of 2.6%. Markets interpreted this as disinflationary. May gasoline prices averaged $3.42/gallon, up 8% from April. However, used car prices fell 2.1% month-over-month according to the Manheim index.
- (cycle 2) Cleveland Fed inflation nowcast for May CPI: 2.85% YoY. This model has had a mean absolute error of 0.12pp over the last 12 months. May shelter cost data from Zillow suggests continued moderation in rent growth, with observed rent index falling 0.3% month-over-month.

{
  "fair_value_estimate": 2.75,
  "bid_price": 2.74,
  "ask_price": 2.76,
  "bid_size": 10,
  "ask_size": 10,
  "reasoning": "Recent hedge fund trades show a tight market around 2.65-2.66, but the Cleveland Fed nowcast (2.85) and Goldman Sachs forecast (2.7) suggest upward pressure. With a short position of -5, I increase the bid to attract sells and cover exposure, while keeping the ask modestly above fair value to avoid adding more short. The bid-ask spread provides liquidity and aligns with the estimated fair value of 2.75."
}

parsed.reasoning: Recent hedge fund trades show a tight market around 2.65-2.66, but the Cleveland Fed nowcast (2.85) and Goldman Sachs forecast (2.7) suggest upward pressure. With a short position of -5, I increase the bid to attract sells and cover exposure, while keeping the ask modestly above fair value to avoid adding more short. The bid-ask spread provides liquidity and aligns with the estimated fair value of 2.75.
